title: Piercing Armour
teaches: AP worsens the target's armour save — and an invulnerable save is the floor that AP can never push past.
intro: Skitarii Rangers hold the line ahead: lightly built troops (Toughness 3) hiding behind a 4+ armour save and a strange 5+ invulnerable field. Your bolt rifles carry AP -1 — armour-piercing that worsens their armour save. Watch the SAVE line: armour bends to AP, but the invulnerable never does.

turns.narrate_before: Open fire — then read the SAVE line closely. Their 4+ armour becomes 5+ under your AP -1, and their 5+ invulnerable sits right at that floor.
Fire again. Same math every time: AP moves the armour save; the invulnerable save never moves.

outro: AP -1 pushed the Rangers' 4+ armour to 5+ — exactly level with their 5+ invulnerable, so the field gained them nothing this time. But that is the whole point of an invulnerable save: it is a floor. Bring AP -2 and the armour would need 6+ while the invulnerable still saves on 5+, and the defender always uses the better of the two. Ask 'why save' in any battle for the full rule.
